signals.raw: On April 8, North Korea fired multiple short-range ballistic missiles from Wonsan after **Trump** announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran, but Japan confirmed no territorial entry and experts attribute it to weapons testing like solid-fuel tech[1][1].

signals.headline: As of April 11, North Korea ran three days of tests showcasing electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons, carbon fiber "blacko
signals.raw: As of April 11, North Korea ran three days of tests showcasing electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons, carbon fiber "blackout" bombs, and cluster warheads for Hwasong-11 missiles (ranges 240-700 km), described by state media as "special assets" for infrastructure disablement; South Korea detected launches amid Iran focus[3][3].
